title: [Episode Title]

sections.heading: Introduction
paragraphs: [SHOW_SIGNON]
[A vivid scenario, provocative question, or relatable moment that immediately connects the listener to the topic emotionally.]
[One or two sentences that name what the episode is and is not. Set the listener's expectation clearly.]
[Tell the listener what they will learn: the 2-3 big ideas and why each matters for their faith or daily life.]
[Briefly name the key scriptures that will anchor the episode. One sentence per passage.]

sections.heading: Topic Introduction
paragraphs: [Step back and reframe how the listener should think about this topic. Correct a common misconception or set up a fresh lens.]
[Show that this topic or tension appears throughout Scripture. Use 2-3 brief examples from the Old and New Testaments.]
[Describe what makes this topic uniquely challenging in the modern world. What pressures or trends make it harder today?]
[If available, include a relevant study, survey, or data point that grounds the cultural challenge in reality. Omit this paragraph if none.]
[Connect everything back to the listener. End with the central question the rest of the episode will answer.]
[State the key concept clearly. Name it, define it, and explain why it matters before going deeper.]
[If helpful, draw a contrast or distinction that prevents the listener from misunderstanding the concept.]
[Integrate a quote or insight from a trusted theologian or Christian author. Format: Author, in Book Title, insight or quote.]
[Introduce the main passage naturally, with a sentence of context before quoting it.]
[Quote the verse(s) in NIV. Format: Book Chapter:Verse — 'full quote' (NIV).]
[Explain what the original audience would have understood that modern readers might miss.]
[What does this biblical truth mean for the listener right now? Offer a specific, practical example or action.]
[State the key concept and connect it to the previous subsection.]
[Name a common wrong response or pitfall before showing the biblical alternative.]
[Point to a specific person or moment in Scripture that demonstrates the right response.]
[Quote the key verse(s) in NIV with a sentence of setup.]
[Add a scholar or author's perspective that deepens or challenges the listener's thinking.]
[Explain anything about the original audience or historical setting that makes the passage land harder.]
[Where relevant, give the listener specific words or actions. What do they actually say or do this week?]
[State the final key concept. This one often lands hardest or requires the most surrender.]
[Name the difficulty honestly. Why is this hard? What does the listener have to let go of?]
[First supporting verse in NIV, with context.]
[Second supporting verse in NIV, with context.]
[If applicable, reference a longer biblical story or parable that illustrates the concept in full.]
[Add a scholar's voice to deepen the theological point.]
[Close with a specific, doable action. What does surrendering to this truth look like in practice this week?]

sections.heading: Conclusion
paragraphs: ['Let me bring this home.' Or a similar phrase that signals the close.]
[Briefly recap the 2-3 most important ideas from the episode. Each idea in one sentence.]
[Restate the central message of the episode in 1-2 clear sentences. The one thing they should walk away knowing.]
[Give them one specific, simple thing to do this week. Make it concrete and doable.]
[Introduce and quote the benediction or closing verse in NIV.]
[One final uplifting sentence that sends the listener off feeling encouraged, not overwhelmed.]
[SHOW_SIGNOFF]

references: [Last, First. (Year). Article title. Journal Name, volume(issue), pages.]
[Author or Organization. (Year, Month Day). Page title. Website Name. URL]
